2010-06-27.abstract: The Czech president said he will appoint a center-right leader, Petr Necas, as prime minister, clearing the way for an economically liberal, pro-American government with a focus on fiscal austerity.

2010-06-27.title: Vettel Dominates European Grand Prix, as Webber Flips Out
2010-06-27.abstract: Sebastian Vettel controlled the European Grand Prix, winning from the pole position, while his teammate Mark Webber crashed after eight laps.

2010-06-28.title: Safety Net Frays in Spain, as Elsewhere in Europe
2010-06-28.abstract: For many Spaniards, the country’s austerity measures — which include salary cuts for civil servants, pension freezes and the elimination of a $3,400 baby bonus — have been profoundly unsettling.

2010-06-28.title: North Korea Rejects U.S. Proposal for Talks on Sinking of Warship
2010-06-28.abstract: North Korea said Sunday that the United Nations Command’s armistice commission had sent it a message calling for general-level talks to inform the North of the results of its investigation.

2010-06-28.title: Lush Land Winds Through a Ravaged Capital
2010-06-28.abstract: A small no man’s land separates a small, besieged enclave controlled by the government from radical insurgents.

2010-06-28.title: Le Monde’s Board to Choose Investors
2010-06-28.abstract: The French newspaper’s board is to decide between a group backed by the paper’s journalists, who now have control, and a group favored by France’s president.

2010-06-28.title: Palestinians and Police Collide in East Jerusalem
2010-06-28.abstract: The city plans to raze 22 Palestinian homes to make way for a tourist area.

2010-06-29.title: Right-to-Know Law Gives India’s Poor a Lever
2010-06-29.abstract: The popular Right to Information law may not have made a big dent in corruption, but it has given a powerful tool to Indians by helping to resolve their complaints.

2010-06-29.title: African Leader Hires Adviser and Seeks an Image Change
2010-06-29.abstract: President Teodoro Obiang Nguema Mbasogo of Equatorial Guinea has hired an American lobbyist to help him recast his reputation as a corrupt, repressive leader in a more progressive mold.

2010-06-29.title: Moving Out One by One as Australia Pursues Coal
2010-06-29.abstract: A 120-year-old town, down to its last home, has become a catalyst for anger over the coal industry’s growth.

2010-06-29.title: 5 Confirmed for Cabinet in Afghanistan
2010-06-29.abstract: This was the third effort by President Hamid Karzai to fill his 25-member cabinet.

2010-06-29.title: Quest to Neutralize Afghan Militants Is Showing Glimpses of Success, NATO Says
2010-06-29.abstract: About 130 important insurgent figures have been captured or killed in Afghanistan over the past 120 days, according to NATO military statistics.

2010-06-30.title: Campaign Intensifies in Iran to Spare a Kurdish Activist
2010-06-30.abstract: As reports circulated that Iran was preparing to execute Zeinab Jalalian, a prominent opposition figure publicly urged the authorities to show compassion.

2010-06-30.title: Safety Car Controversy Continues Long After European Grand Prix
2010-06-30.abstract: The controversy continues after the European Grand Prix as drivers, teams, fans and journalists take sides on the safety car controversy.

2010-06-30.title: At Russia-China Border, Bear Paws Sell Best
2010-06-30.abstract: The border between Russia and China has been creaking open, clearing the way for a cross-border criminal enterprise in animal parts for Chinese medicine and cooking.

2010-06-30.title: In Rewriting Its History, Iraq Treads Cautiously
2010-06-30.abstract: Among other sensitive subjects, Saddam Hussein and the 2003 American invasion have been skipped over as Iraq revises its curriculum.

2010-06-30.title: Twitter Musings in Syria Elicit Groans in Washington
2010-06-30.abstract: Two young State Department employees in Syria embarrassed the department with a series of breezy Twitter posts, highlighting the risks of using social media as a tool for diplomacy.
